abstract: Initializes a Gaussian random distribution with the specified lower and upper bounds, using the specified source randomizer.

declaration.code: init(randomSource source: any GKRandom, lowestValue lowestInclusive: Int, highestValue highestInclusive: Int)

overview:
## Return Value

A new random distribution.

## Discussion

Using this initializer creates a Gaussian distribution centered within the specified range. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/mean] property is set to the midpoint between the `lowestInclusive` and `highestInclusive` parameters (`mean = (highest + lowest) / 2`), and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/deviation] property is set such that the highest and lowest values are each three deviations away from the mean (`deviation = (highest - lowest) / 6`). The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/mean] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/deviation] properties can thus be floating-point values even if you use the distribution only to produce integers.

A random distribution works by mapping the values produced by the `source` randomizer to the range and characteristics specified by the distribution. Multiple distributions can share the same source, with the side effect that retrieving a random value through one distribution affects the sequence of random numbers produced by the source.

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

# init(randomSource:lowestValue:highestValue:)

**Initializer**

Initializes a Gaussian random distribution with the specified lower and upper bounds, using the specified source randomizer.

## Declaration

```swift
init(randomSource source: any GKRandom, lowestValue lowestInclusive: Int, highestValue highestInclusive: Int)
```

## Parameters

- **source**: A randomizer that produces raw random values for use by the distribution. A randomizer is any object implementing the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandom] protocol, which can be a random source algorithm such as the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKARC4RandomSource] class or another random distribution.
- **lowestInclusive**: The lowest value to be produced by the distribution.
- **highestInclusive**: The highest value to be produced by the distribution.

## Return Value

A new random distribution.

## Discussion

Using this initializer creates a Gaussian distribution centered within the specified range. The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/mean] property is set to the midpoint between the `lowestInclusive` and `highestInclusive` parameters (`mean = (highest + lowest) / 2`), and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/deviation] property is set such that the highest and lowest values are each three deviations away from the mean (`deviation = (highest - lowest) / 6`). The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/mean] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGaussianDistribution/deviation] properties can thus be floating-point values even if you use the distribution only to produce integers.

A random distribution works by mapping the values produced by the `source` randomizer to the range and characteristics specified by the distribution. Multiple distributions can share the same source, with the side effect that retrieving a random value through one distribution affects the sequence of random numbers produced by the source.

For more information, see [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

## Creating a Random Distribution

- **init(randomSource:mean:deviation:)**: Initializes a Gaussian random distribution with the specified mean and deviation, using the specified source randomizer.
